sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव।
स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम्॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
sthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣā samādhi-sthasya keśava
sthita-dhīḥ kiṃ prabhāṣeta kim āsīta vrajeta kim
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: What is the speech of one established in steady wisdom, of one merged in samādhi, O Keśava? How does the poised intellect speak, how sit, how walk?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna shifts from despair to curiosity, asking for observable markers of inner integration so he can recognize and emulate the stabilized mind.
psychological_state: Post-crisis inquiry—moving from paralysis to constructive curiosity about embodied wisdom.
trigger_scenario: After a panic attack, asking the therapist 'What does a calm person actually look like in daily life?'
anchor_text: Watch how calm minds speak, sit, and move, then copy their steady ease.
